# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an Istio configuration that enables Envoy admin interface on all sidecars. Set proxyAdminPort to 15000 and make it accessible for debugging. Expose admin endpoints like /config_dump, /stats, and /clusters on all pods. This is for troubleshooting service mesh issues in production.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: envoy-admin-gateway
spec:
  selector:
    istio: ingress-gateway   # This gateway only handles requests from the Istio Ingress Gateways, not external traffic or services.
  servers:
  - port:
      number: 15000           # The admin interface listens on this port for debugging and other operations like config dump etc..
    name: http2              # This is the listener that will handle all requests from clients, it uses HTTP/2 protocol.  
---    
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3 
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: envoy-admin-virtualservice   
spec:
  hosts:                           # This virtual service is applied to all pods in the namespace where it's defined, ie., 'default'.  
  - "*"                           
  gateways:                        # The gateway that this VirtualService should route requests from. In our case we are using ingress-gateway which handles external traffic and services only (not internal).   
  - envoy-admin-gateway            
  http:                           # This section defines how the client sends HTTP/2 request to Envoy admin server, it uses 'envoy' as proxy.  
  - route:                         # The list of routes that this VirtualService should follow when sending a new connection from clients (like curl command).   
      - destination: envoy-admin     # This is the name or ID of DestinationRule to which requests are sent if no other rule matches, in our case 'envoy-admin'.
